title: Best AppSumo Deals in 2026: Lifetime Software Actually Worth Buying
desc: The best AppSumo lifetime deals, re-verified live on July 14, 2026: top picks for agencies and lead gen, what sold out since June, and the checklist that filters out duds.
keywords: best appsumo deals, best appsumo deals 2026, appsumo lifetime deals, are appsumo lifetime deals worth it, best appsumo deals for agencies, appsumo plus worth it
cta: Want a lead-gen stack that actually converts?
body:
Quick answer: AppSumo lifetime deals are still legit in 2026, and they are still the cheapest way to build a software stack for an agency or a small business. You pay once, usually $49 to $99, instead of a monthly subscription. The platform has run for 16 years, serves over 2 million customers, and backs practically every purchase with a 60-day money-back guarantee. The catch is product survival, and rotation. When we refreshed this page on July 14, 2026, three of the seven picks from our June list were already gone. So the game is simple. Buy proven tools with strong ratings and real review volume, test them hard inside the 60-day refund window, and never buy on roadmap promises. Below are the picks that pass that filter, re-verified live on AppSumo today, plus the exact checklist we use before spending a dollar.

What AppSumo is and why the deals are so cheap

AppSumo is the largest marketplace for software lifetime deals. Early-stage SaaS companies list their product at a steep one-time price, often 75 to 95 percent below the equivalent yearly subscription, in exchange for a burst of users, cash flow, and feedback. You get lifetime access to the plan you bought. The vendor gets distribution. AppSumo takes a cut.

That trade explains both the upside and the risk. The discount is real because the vendor is buying growth, not running a charity. But some of those vendors will pivot, get acquired, or shut down, and a lifetime deal only lasts as long as the product does. AppSumo reduces the risk in three ways: a 60-day money-back guarantee on practically everything, an AppSumo Select badge for products the team vets and partners with directly, and public review scores from verified buyers, rated out of five tacos.

How to pick a lifetime deal that lasts

We apply five filters before buying any deal. They will not catch every dud, but they remove most of them.

  • Rating of 4.5 or higher with at least 50 reviews. A 4.9 with 12 reviews is noise. A 4.7 with 300 reviews is signal.
  • The product has been on the market 12 months or more. Most lifetime-deal failures happen early. A tool that has shipped updates for a year or two is far more likely to be around in year five.
  • An active changelog or public roadmap. If the last founder update was eight months ago, assume the team has moved on.
  • Buy for what the tool does today. Roadmap promises are marketing. If the current feature set does not justify the price, pass.
  • Stress-test inside the 60-day window. Import real data, run a real campaign, hit the limits of your tier. Refund without guilt if it disappoints.

One more rule that saves money: check whether the deal is tiered. Most listings sell License Tier 1, 2, and 3, or sell stackable codes. Buy the lowest tier that covers your usage, because you can usually upgrade later while the deal is live, and tier limits are where cheap deals quietly become expensive.

Browse the current AppSumo deals here

The best live AppSumo deals for agencies and lead gen, July 2026

We re-verified every pick below directly on AppSumo on July 14, 2026. Prices are the standing one-time prices for the entry tier, and availability is current as of that date. Deals rotate fast, so if one is gone when you look, the checklist above still applies to whatever replaced it.

  • LeadRocks, $79 one-time (about 95 percent off a $1,740 list). Rated 4.5 from 367 reviews, and AppSumo Select. A B2B contact database with 100M+ records, plus emails, phone numbers, and LinkedIn profiles, a Chrome extension, CSV and CRM export, and LinkedIn URL enrichment. The $79 tier gives you 5,000 contact credits per month, which is a genuinely large allowance at this price. For an agency or a sales team that wants a cheap, owned prospecting list, this is still the standout lead-gen deal. The honest catches: email verification credits and the outreach seat only start at the 2-code $158 tier, and like every low-cost database, accuracy varies, so treat the output as a starting list and verify before you send.
  • Cleanlist AI, $79 one-time. Rated 4.71 from 62 reviews. It finds verified emails and direct phone numbers using a waterfall across 15+ data providers, which is the same technique expensive enrichment platforms sell at ten times the price. Buy LeadRocks for the raw list and Cleanlist for the hard-to-find contacts, or use it to clean a list you already own.
  • FlexiFunnels, $49 one-time (regularly $199). Rated 4.64 from 88 reviews. AI-built landing pages, funnels, course pages, and a built-in LMS from a prompt, with checkout features included. Pair it with a cold email engine and you have a complete top-of-funnel for under $100.
  • Letterly, $89 one-time (regularly $200). Rated 4.69 from 349 reviews, and AppSumo Select. Speech-to-structured-text in any app, on every device, with unlimited dictation and no word caps. Founders who think out loud use it to turn voice notes into proposals, follow-up emails, and LinkedIn posts. It now connects to Claude and ChatGPT over MCP, which is a real 2026 upgrade rather than a marketing line.
  • Stackby, $99 one-time. Rated 4.65 from 144 reviews. Spreadsheet-style databases with automations, which is the cheapest honest replacement for an Airtable subscription. Good for client trackers, content calendars, and lead pipelines that do not justify a real CRM yet.
  • Poppy AI, $279 one-time. Rated 4.88 from 141 reviews, the highest-rated tool with real review volume on the platform right now. It pulls YouTube videos, PDFs, and voice notes onto one canvas and writes on-brand scripts, posts, and emails from them. It is the priciest pick here by a distance, so only buy it if content production is a real bottleneck for you.

Proof that deals rotate: what happened to our June list

This is the part most roundups quietly skip. When we last refreshed this page in late June, we named seven picks. Eighteen days later, on July 14, three of them are unbuyable:

  • Snoooz ($39, AI email assistant, 4.86 from 88 reviews) now shows Sold out with a notify-me button.
  • Mini Course Generator ($99, 4.75 from 222 reviews) is also Sold out, despite being one of the platform's most established listings.
  • Pretty Prompt ($34) has been delisted entirely. Its product page no longer exists.
  • Heffl ($59 CRM) is running its ending-soon countdown at zero, so treat it as on the way out.

That is the lifetime-deal market in one paragraph. Strong deals disappear, often at the peak of their reviews, and the good ones rarely come back at the same price. The practical lesson: when a deal clears the five-filter checklist, buy it and use the 60-day refund window as your trial. Waiting for a better moment usually costs you the deal, not the money.

See what is live on AppSumo right now

On the watchlist: high ratings, not enough reviews yet

Three deals live today are interesting but fail our 50-review filter, so we are watching rather than recommending. ZeroRank AI ($69, 4.96 from 24 reviews) monitors whether AI assistants cite your brand, which is exactly where search is heading. SnowSEO ($79, 4.72 from 18 reviews) bundles keyword research, content generation, and rank tracking for Google and AI search. Respona ($79, 5.0 from 2 reviews) chases backlinks on the listicles and review pages that LLMs cite. All three sit in a category we work in daily, and all three are too new to judge. If you buy them, buy them as a 60-day experiment, not as infrastructure.

The always-available picks: AppSumo's own tools

AppSumo also builds and owns a small family of products: TidyCal for scheduling, SendFox for email newsletters, BreezeDoc for e-signatures, FormRobin for forms, and KingSumo for giveaways. These are permanent listings rather than rotating deals, and they carry the lowest survival risk on the platform, because AppSumo is not going to shut down its own products. TidyCal in particular has become the default Calendly replacement for lifetime-deal buyers. If you want zero-drama utility software for the boring parts of your stack, start there.

When to buy: Sumo Day, Black Friday, and AppSumo Plus

Timing matters more than most buyers realize. AppSumo drops new deals every week, but a few times a year it runs sitewide sales where everything gets cheaper. Sumo Day, its biggest annual event, ran in early June 2026 and has wrapped, so the next big moment on the calendar is Black Friday in November, historically the deepest discount of the year. You rarely need to wait, though. New deals land weekly, the strong ones sell out, and the 60-day refund means buying today carries little risk.

The other lever is AppSumo Plus at $99 per year. Verified today: members get 10 percent off every order, $100 in coupons paid out as $25 every 90 days, early access and extended access around Sumo Day and Black Friday, free License Tier 1 of KingSumo and SendFox while the membership lasts, and stronger purchase protection. That last one is the part worth reading twice: if an AppSumo Select tool goes out of business within a year of purchase, Plus members get 100 percent back in credits, while non-members get 50 percent.

The honest counterweight: Plus is rated 3.8 from 309 reviews, the weakest score of anything on this page, and the membership is non-refundable. The math still works if you are buying a stack. Ignore the coupons and the discount alone needs about $990 of annual spend to pay for itself. Count the $100 in coupons and it roughly pays for itself the moment you use them. A founder buying one tool a year should skip it. An agency assembling a full stack this quarter should join for that quarter and cancel before renewal.

The honest downsides nobody puts in the headline

We earn a commission if you buy through our links, so take this section as the proof that we want you buying well, not buying often.

  • Lifetime means the product's lifetime, not yours. If the vendor shuts down, your deal dies with it. This is the single biggest risk, and it is why the checklist above exists.
  • Tier limits bite later. The $79 tier that covers you today may not cover you at twice the volume, and stacking codes after the deal closes is not always possible.
  • Support is rarely subscription-grade. You paid once, and some vendors quietly prioritize monthly subscribers in the support queue.
  • Data and feature gaps versus category leaders are real. A $79 contact database is not ZoomInfo, a $99 spreadsheet database is not a CRM, and a $49 funnel builder is not ClickFunnels. For core revenue infrastructure, sometimes the subscription is the right call.
  • Sold out means sold out. Three of our June picks proved it in under three weeks. Hesitating on a strong deal usually costs more than a refunded experiment.

Building a real stack: where lifetime deals fit

The smartest way to use AppSumo is to cover the supporting layer of your business for a few hundred dollars, one time, and keep your recurring spend for the systems that directly produce revenue. Scheduling, forms, documents, internal databases, and content tooling are perfect lifetime-deal territory. The pipeline itself, meaning the system that finds prospects, reaches them, and books calls, is usually worth doing properly.

That is the part we build for clients every day. If you want the pipeline handled end to end, our team runs done-for-you B2B lead generation and cold email campaigns that plug straight into whatever stack you assemble, lifetime deals included. Buy the $79 contact database on AppSumo, and spend what you saved on the system that turns it into booked calls.

Bottom line: AppSumo in 2026 rewards disciplined buyers. Use the five-filter checklist, favor proven tools with real review volume, treat the 60-day guarantee as a free trial, and act when a deal clears the bar, because the June list proves it will not wait for you.

faqs: Are AppSumo lifetime deals worth it in 2026?

Yes, for the supporting layer of your stack. A $49 to $99 one-time payment against a $200 to $1,700 yearly list price pays for itself quickly, and the 60-day money-back guarantee means a bad buy costs you time rather than money. The risk is vendor survival, not the platform: independent trackers estimate a large share of lifetime-deal products fade within a few years. Buy tools rated 4.5 or higher with at least 50 reviews, and do not put core revenue infrastructure on a lifetime deal.
Is AppSumo legit and safe to buy from?

AppSumo has operated for 16 years and serves more than 2 million customers. Every deal carries a 60-day money-back guarantee, and AppSumo Select products are vetted by the AppSumo team. If a Select tool goes out of business within a year of your purchase, you get 50 percent back in credits, or 100 percent if you are an AppSumo Plus member. The platform is safe. The individual vendors are the variable.
What are the best AppSumo deals right now?

Verified live on July 14, 2026: LeadRocks at $79 for a 100M+ record B2B contact database (4.5 from 367 reviews, AppSumo Select), Cleanlist AI at $79 for verified emails and direct phone numbers, FlexiFunnels at $49 for AI landing pages and funnels, Letterly at $89 for unlimited voice-to-text, Stackby at $99 for spreadsheet databases with automations, and Poppy AI at $279 for AI content production. Deals rotate weekly, so check availability before you plan around one.
Is AppSumo Plus worth $99 a year?

It depends on your spend. Plus gives you 10 percent off every order plus $100 in coupons paid as $25 every 90 days, extended access to Sumo Day and Black Friday, and 100 percent purchase protection on Select tools instead of 50 percent. On the discount alone you need about $990 of annual spend to break even. Counting the coupons, it roughly pays for itself once you use them. It is non-refundable and rated only 3.8 from 309 reviews, so join for a quarter when you are building a stack, not by default.
Why did the AppSumo deal I wanted disappear?

Lifetime deals are limited runs. Vendors cap how many codes they sell, and AppSumo closes the listing when the cap is hit or the campaign window ends. Between our late-June and mid-July checks, Snoooz and Mini Course Generator sold out and Pretty Prompt was delisted entirely. If a deal clears your checklist, buy it and use the 60-day refund window as the trial. Waiting rarely gets you a better price.
Can I get a refund on an AppSumo purchase?

Yes. Practically every deal is refundable for 60 days, no questions asked, which is why we treat the window as a paid trial: import real data, run a real workflow, and hit the limits of your tier before day 60. The main exception is AppSumo Plus itself, which is a non-refundable subscription, although you can cancel before it renews.
